content.problemStatement:
Chef Two and Chef Ten are playing a game with a number XXX. In one turn, they can multiply XXX by 222. The goal of the game is to make XXX divisible by 101010.

Help the Chefs find the smallest number of turns necessary to win the game (it may be possible to win in zero turns) or determine that it is impossible.
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows. The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer denoting the initial value of XXX.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the minimum required number of turns or −1 if there is no way to win the game.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the initial value of XXX is 10, which is already divisible by 101010. Therefore, the minimum number of turns required is 0. For the second test case, the initial value of XXX is 25, which is not divisible by 101010. However, after one turn, the value of XXX becomes 5550, which is divisible by 101010. Therefore, the minimum number of turns required is 1. For the third test case, the initial value of XXX is 1, which is not divisible by 101010. Moreover, no matter how many turns we perform, the value of XXX will never become divisible by 101010. Therefore, the output is -1.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we need to find the smallest number of turns required to make XXX divisible by 101010. We can achieve this by multiplying XXX by 222 in each turn. We can use modular arithmetic to keep track of the remainder of XXX when divided by 101010. If the remainder becomes 0, we have found the minimum number of turns required. If the remainder does not become 0 after a certain number of turns, we can conclude that it is impossible to win the game.
solution.timeComplexity: O(log X) — because we are multiplying XXX by 222 in each turn, which is equivalent to shifting the bits of XXX to the left by 1 position in each turn. The number of turns required is proportional to the number of bits in the binary representation of X.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — because we only need to keep track of the remainder of XXX when divided by 101010, which requires a constant amount of space.
